# Session Bootstrap Correctness Remediation Handoff

Resume the durable session-bootstrap implementation track from the persisted handoff state. Actions 1–7 produced substantial functionality, but independent review found correctness holes in finish authority, live ticket resolution, Windows durability, post-finish behavior, evidence traceability, and CLI contract alignment. Repair these in order before starting action 8 or closing the epic.

## Durable Session Identity

- `workspace_session_id`: `448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0`
- Outgoing `run_id`: `9484f7af-c5dd-422c-9c03-037b6a760e1a`
- `handoff_id`: `82038269-ec3d-4eee-8f3c-e091d40d255b`
- Runtime schema version: `1`
- Persisted context: [./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/context.json](./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/context.json)
- Persisted handoff: [./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/handoffs/82038269-ec3d-4eee-8f3c-e091d40d255b.json](./.session/runtime/workspaces/448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0/handoffs/82038269-ec3d-4eee-8f3c-e091d40d255b.json)
- Exact resume command:

```bash
session-cli resume --workspace-session-id 448a0119-dd96-4213-98e9-92bf27a444d0 --predecessor-run-id 9484f7af-c5dd-422c-9c03-037b6a760e1a
```

Start in the repository root:

```bash
cd /c/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine
```

## Long-Horizon Goal

Complete [effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 [session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8/ticket.toml) with durable pinned context, authoritative workflow state, terminal and Mermaid rendering, reliable handoff/resume continuity, and graph-gated completion that cannot be certified by caller-controlled or stale local data.

Do not begin [b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6 [session-bootstrap][rules] Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml) until the remediation workflow below passes.

## Key Entities

- [effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 [session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8/ticket.toml): owning epic.
- [b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6 [session-bootstrap][rules] Minimal bootstrapper + selective instruction loading](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml): action 8, currently blocked.
- [0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4 [session-api][handoff] Persist handoff records and resume durable session workspaces](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4/ticket.toml): prior handoff implementation ticket; direct lookup reports `done`.
- [70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6 [session-api] Durable session workflow persistence and mutation](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6/ticket.toml): prior persistence implementation ticket; direct lookup reports `done`.
- [6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1 [session-api] Add init/pin/unpin/view to session-cli and session-mcp](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1/ticket.toml): prior transport implementation ticket; direct lookup reports `done`.
- [8c880efc Durable session bootstrapping contract](./.spec/specs/8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913/spec.toml): parent contract and implementation positions.
- [c677182e Durable workflow contract](./.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/spec.toml): workflow persistence, rendering, handoff, and finish contract.
- [val-session-workflow-finish](./.test/default/specs/val-session-workflow-finish.json): required finish validation definition.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs): finish, validation resolution, ticket resolution, workflow snapshot, and mutation behavior.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs): JSON replacement and durability behavior.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs): existing tests and missing adversarial coverage.
- [./memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs): CLI transport and hierarchy.
- [./memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs): MCP finish and handoff transport.
- [./.agents/prompts/handoff.prompt.md](./.agents/prompts/handoff.prompt.md): generated handoff guidance backed by canonical rule `084fd4e6-660b-4227-a13e-514edf44e393`.

## Decisions Already Made

- `workspace_session_id` remains durable across executions.
- Every resumed execution receives a distinct `run_id` linked through `predecessor_run_id`.
- Ticket-api is authoritative for ticket lifecycle state.
- Test-api execution records are authoritative for validation outcomes.
- Caller payloads may identify or display validation gates but must not certify outcomes.
- Required ticket nodes must fail closed when live state is missing or unavailable.
- Temporary workflow nodes may use local status; ticket-backed nodes may not override live ticket state.
- Handoff records must persist before rendering and expose structured record data, path, and exact resume command.
- Action 8 remains blocked until all required remediation and evidence nodes pass.
- Feedback emission remains optional and cannot gate core session behavior.

## Verified Repairs From The Previous Pass

- Dependency blocker direction was corrected.
- CLI and MCP handoff results now expose the structured record and path.
- Empty URN segments and invalid ticket-node kinds are rejected.
- Validation command filters now match actual test names.
- Parent spec positions were updated.
- Editor diagnostics reported no errors.

Do not spend time reopening these unless new validation demonstrates a regression.

## Remaining Findings

### Critical: Caller-Controlled Validation Still Overrides Test-API

In `resolve_validation_gates`, a caller-provided gate already present in `by_id` supplies the final outcome. Test-api is queried only when the caller omits that gate. A caller can submit `passed` while the latest authoritative execution failed or does not exist.

Required behavior:

- Always fetch required guard outcomes from test-api.
- Never accept caller-provided outcomes as completion authority.
- Fail closed for unknown validation specs, absent executions, failed executions, and blocked executions.
- Add a regression test where the caller submits `passed` while authoritative evidence is failed or missing; finish must reject it.

### Critical: Local `Done` Still Overrides Live Ticket State

`node_is_effectively_done` returns true immediately for any locally `Done` node before considering whether it is ticket-backed. A live ticket in `new`, `ready`, `in-implementation`, `in-review`, missing, or unresolved can therefore be treated as complete.

Required behavior:

- For ticket-backed nodes, derive completion exclusively from authoritative live terminal state.
- Local status may be display/cache information but cannot certify completion.
- Add a regression test where a ticket node is locally `Done` while the resolver reports a non-terminal state; finish must reject it.

### High: Missing Or Misrouted Ticket State Can Fail Open

The default resolver returns `Ok(None)` when a ticket is absent. The workflow snapshot records this as a normal resolution rather than an unavailable-state diagnostic. The parsed URN workspace segment is also ignored while one sibling ticket store is queried.

Required behavior:

- Treat missing required ticket state as unavailable and block finish.
- Route cross-workspace ticket URNs to the addressed store or reject unsupported workspace routing explicitly.
- Ensure diagnostics themselves cannot be ignored by finish.
- Add production-path tests using the real default resolver and actual store layout.

### High: Windows Replacement Is Not Atomic

The Windows branch removes the valid destination before renaming the temporary file. A crash or failed rename after deletion loses the old durable state.

Required behavior:

- Use a Windows replacement strategy that preserves the old file until replacement succeeds.
- Define and enforce concurrent mutation behavior with locking, version checks, compare-and-swap, or another established repository pattern.
- Add focused failure and concurrency tests where practical.

### High: Finished Workspaces Can Become Stale

`finish_workflow` returns an existing `finish.json` before reevaluating workflow, ticket, or validation state. Workflow mutation APIs remain available afterward. A finished workspace can become incomplete while continuing to return stale success.

Required behavior:

- Choose and document one invariant:
  - finished workspaces are immutable, or
  - any mutation invalidates/removes the finish record and a later finish reevaluates all authorities.
- Add a regression test for mutation after finish.

### High: Validation Evidence Is Missing

The owning specs cite `exec-val-session-*` execution IDs, but no corresponding execution files were found under [./.test/default/executions](./.test/default/executions). Passing local tests without persisted execution records does not satisfy traceability.

Required behavior:

- Run and record each required validation through the repository workflow/test-api path.
- Ensure every cited execution ID resolves to an actual execution record.
- Update spec evidence references if generated IDs differ.
- Investigate unrelated deleted historical execution records before staging or committing them. Do not restore or delete unrelated evidence blindly.

### Medium: CLI Hierarchy Does Not Match The Contract

The CLI still exposes flat commands such as `workflow-add-node` and `workflow-render-terminal` while the intended public shape is nested under `session workflow`.

Required behavior:

- Implement the documented nested hierarchy.
- Preserve flat forms as compatibility aliases when feasible.
- Add CLI parsing/integration tests for both canonical and compatibility forms.

## Ticket And Index Warning

A focused ticket search found no existing ticket specifically covering validation authority, live ticket authority, atomic replacement, and stale finish semantics. Before editing code:

1. Search once more using the resumed session’s live ticket tools.
2. Create a focused remediation child ticket under the owning epic if no match exists.
3. Update the relevant spec positions from `implemented` to `partial` where these defects invalidate the contract.
4. Check in on the draftboard with a narrow file list.

There is also an index inconsistency to resolve: direct ticket lookups report the completed implementation tickets above as `done`, while a root-scoped `ticket next` traversal reported some of the same tickets as `ready` or `new`. Run a force scan and health check before relying on dependency readiness:

```bash
rtk ./target/debug/ticket.exe scan --force --toon
rtk ./target/debug/ticket.exe health effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 --toon
rtk ./target/debug/ticket.exe next effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 --toon
```

The board was clear at handoff time: `0/5` active, no stale entries, no conflicts, and no owned files.

## Persisted Workflow

The durable handoff contains this required blocker chain:

1. `authoritative-validation`
2. `live-ticket-authority`, blocked by `authoritative-validation`
3. `durable-finish-semantics`, blocked by `live-ticket-authority`
4. `evidence-repair`, blocked by `durable-finish-semantics`
5. `cli-contract`, blocked by `evidence-repair`
6. `action-8`, blocked by `cli-contract`

All six nodes are persisted as `pending`. The handoff records one live resolution: action 8 is currently `new`. The required validation node references `val-session-workflow-finish`, whose handoff outcome is unresolved.

## Next Actions

1. Resume the durable session with the exact command above.
2. Reconcile the ticket index, run epic health, search for a remediation ticket, and create one if absent.
3. Check in on the remediation ticket before editing.
4. Update the owning spec positions and ticket traceability to reflect the still-partial finish contract.
5. Remove caller-controlled validation authority and add the adversarial regression test.
6. Run the narrow validation immediately:

```bash
cd memory-api
rtk cargo test -p session-api workflow_finish
```

7. Make ticket-backed completion exclusively live-state based, fail closed on missing resolution, and add resolver tests.
8. Define post-finish mutation semantics and implement safe Windows replacement plus concurrency protection.
9. Run the complete session-api suite:

```bash
rtk cargo test -p session-api
```

10. Align the nested CLI hierarchy and compatibility aliases, then run focused CLI and MCP tests.
11. Record validation executions through the repository test/workflow tooling and update exact evidence references.
12. Re-run ticket health, inspect the diff for unrelated execution deletions, and move the remediation ticket through `in-review` only after all checks pass.
13. Update each durable workflow node as it completes.
14. Begin action 8 only when every preceding required node is complete and authoritative finish succeeds.

## Completion Status

- `authoritative-validation`: `not-started` — caller-supplied `passed` can still bypass test-api.
- `live-ticket-authority`: `blocked` — execute after validation authority; local `Done` still overrides live ticket state.
- `durable-finish-semantics`: `blocked` — execute after ticket authority; Windows replacement and stale finish remain unresolved.
- `evidence-repair`: `blocked` — requires corrected finish semantics and passing tests.
- `cli-contract`: `blocked` — follows correctness and evidence work.
- `action-8`: `blocked` — do not start until every remediation node passes.
- Epic closure: `blocked`.

## Definition Of Done

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions.
- Caller-supplied outcomes cannot produce successful finish.
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state.
- Missing, failed, or misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish.
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails and concurrent updates cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- All focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence.
- Unrelated execution deletions are explained or excluded from the change.
- Specs, tickets, validation records, and implementation positions agree.
- The remediation ticket reaches `in-review` through the required workflow.
- Action 8 becomes unblocked only after the durable workflow gates are complete.
- The bootstrap epic remains open until action 8 and all remaining children are validated.

Execute these actions in order and continue until every item is completed or explicitly blocked with persisted evidence and an updated handoff.

- `cli-contract`: `blocked` — follows correctness and evidence work.
- `action-8`: `blocked` — do not start until every remediation node passes.
- Epic closure: `blocked`.

## Definition Of Done

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions.
- Caller-supplied outcomes cannot produce successful finish.
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state.
- Missing, failed, or misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish.
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails and concurrent updates cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- All focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence.
- Unrelated execution deletions are explained or excluded from the change.
- Specs, tickets, validation records, and implementation positions agree.
- The remediation ticket reaches `in-review` through the required workflow.
- Action 8 becomes unblocked only after the durable workflow gates are complete.
- The bootstrap epic remains open until action 8 and all remaining children are validated.

Execute these actions in order and continue until every item is completed or explicitly blocked with persisted evidence and an updated handoff.

arguments.content:
# Session bootstrap correctness remediation

Repairs correctness holes found during independent review of the durable
session-bootstrap implementation (actions 1–7) before action 8 or epic closure.

## Findings to repair (in order)

1. **Authoritative validation** — `resolve_validation_gates` lets a caller-provided
   gate outcome win when present in `by_id`. Required guard outcomes must always come
   from test-api; caller payloads may identify/display gates but never certify them.
   Fail closed for unknown specs, absent, failed, or blocked executions.
2. **Live ticket authority** — `node_is_effectively_done` returns true for any locally
   `Done` node before checking whether it is ticket-backed. Ticket-backed nodes must
   derive completion exclusively from authoritative live terminal state; local status is
   display/cache only.
3. **Missing/misrouted ticket state fails open** — default resolver returns `Ok(None)`
   for absent tickets and the URN workspace segment is ignored. Treat missing required
   ticket state as unavailable (block finish); route cross-workspace URNs or reject
   unsupported routing explicitly.
4. **Windows atomic replacement** — the Windows branch deletes the destination before
   renaming the temp file, losing durable state on crash. Preserve the old file until
   replacement succeeds; enforce concurrent-mutation safety.
5. **Stale finish** — `finish_workflow` returns an existing `finish.json` before
   reevaluating authorities, and mutation APIs remain available afterward. Choose and
   enforce one invariant (immutable-after-finish OR mutation invalidates finish).
6. **Evidence traceability** — spec-cited `exec-val-session-*` IDs have no persisted
   execution records. Record required validations through test-api; reconcile cited IDs.
7. **CLI contract** — expose nested `session workflow ...` hierarchy with flat forms as
   compatibility aliases; add parsing/integration tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions; caller
  `passed` cannot produce a successful finish (regression test).
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state; locally `Done` + non-terminal
  live state rejects finish (regression test).
- Missing/failed/misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish (production-path tests).
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails; concurrent updates
  cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence; unrelated execution
  deletions are explained or excluded.
- Focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.

## Traceability

- Epic: effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8
- Blocks action 8: b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6
- Spec: 8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913 (parent contract)

arguments.content:
# Session bootstrap correctness remediation

Repairs correctness holes found during independent review of the durable
session-bootstrap implementation (actions 1–7) before action 8 or epic closure.

## Findings to repair (in order)

1. **Authoritative validation** — `resolve_validation_gates` lets a caller-provided
   gate outcome win when present in `by_id`. Required guard outcomes must always come
   from test-api; caller payloads may identify/display gates but never certify them.
   Fail closed for unknown specs, absent, failed, or blocked executions.
2. **Live ticket authority** — `node_is_effectively_done` returns true for any locally
   `Done` node before checking whether it is ticket-backed. Ticket-backed nodes must
   derive completion exclusively from authoritative live terminal state; local status is
   display/cache only.
3. **Missing/misrouted ticket state fails open** — default resolver returns `Ok(None)`
   for absent tickets and the URN workspace segment is ignored. Treat missing required
   ticket state as unavailable (block finish); route cross-workspace URNs or reject
   unsupported routing explicitly.
4. **Windows atomic replacement** — the Windows branch deletes the destination before
   renaming the temp file, losing durable state on crash. Preserve the old file until
   replacement succeeds; enforce concurrent-mutation safety.
5. **Stale finish** — `finish_workflow` returns an existing `finish.json` before
   reevaluating authorities, and mutation APIs remain available afterward. Choose and
   enforce one invariant (immutable-after-finish OR mutation invalidates finish).
6. **Evidence traceability** — spec-cited `exec-val-session-*` IDs have no persisted
   execution records. Record required validations through test-api; reconcile cited IDs.
7. **CLI contract** — expose nested `session workflow ...` hierarchy with flat forms as
   compatibility aliases; add parsing/integration tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions; caller
  `passed` cannot produce a successful finish (regression test).
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state; locally `Done` + non-terminal
  live state rejects finish (regression test).
- Missing/failed/misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish (production-path tests).
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails; concurrent updates
  cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence; unrelated execution
  deletions are explained or excluded.
- Focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.

## Traceability

- Epic: effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8
- Blocks action 8: b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6

- Spec: c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf (workflow/finish contract)
- Validation: val-session-workflow-finish

events.reasoning_text:
There are 6 deleted execution records from unrelated work that the handoff flagged—these shouldn't be blindly restored or removed since they're historical deletions unrelated to the current session.

These are unstaged deletions that I need to exclude from my commits. Per the guidelines, I shouldn't revert or stage unrelated changes from other agents, and I shouldn't restore them blindly either since they might be intentional. I'll leave them untouched and only commit my own changes.

Now moving into the actual code work.

For the first critical fix in store.rs, I need to change resolve_validation_gates to always query the test-api for required specs instead of accepting the caller's cached outcome. I'm replacing the logic that checks for existing outcomes with a direct query that fetches the latest execution from test_store, then extracts the validation outcome label. The fail-closed behavior is already correct — when strict_required is true and the outcome is missing or not "passed", it returns an error, which properly blocks on absent or failed executions.

The key insight is that this fix applies to both strict and non-strict modes: we always want authoritative outcomes from test-api for required specs, never caller-claimed ones. Non-required caller gates remain informational and stay in the cache for display purposes.

Now I'm moving to the second fix: node_is_effectively_done needs to derive ticket-backed nodes from live state only, not from stale node status.

Refining the logic...

The key insight is that when a ticket is missing (resolver returns Ok(None)), it gets recorded as a normal resolution with no state, so the node correctly blocks as not-done. The real issue is that finish currently ignores diagnostics — it should treat missing required ticket state as a blocker and ensure diagnostics themselves can't be bypassed. I could convert Ok(None) to a diagnostic in the workflow snapshot instead, but the cleaner fix is to make the default resolver distinguish between absent tickets and present-but-stateless ones. Since get_indexed returns Option<IndexedTicket> where None means absent, I should handle that case explicitly in the resolver rather than collapsing both scenarios into None.

Writing resolver logic...

Then in finish_workflow, I need to block completion for required ticket nodes that have diagnostics or missing state. I'll iterate through the nodes and for any required ticket-backed node, check that live_states contains a terminal state — if not, block with an "unavailable" reason.

For the URN workspace routing issue, the safest approach is to explicitly handle cross-workspace ticket URNs: either route them to the correct store or reject them outright rather than silently querying the default store.

Since the session store's workspace is `default` and ticket URNs follow the pattern `ce://default/ticket/...`, matching on `default` works fine. For non-default workspaces, I should reject the request. The aggregated root `.ticket` store can resolve tickets from descendant stores via `include_descendants`, so the memory-api lookup should work correctly through that aggregated index.

I'll add a workspace_slug check in the DefaultTicketStateResolver—if the parsed URN's workspace doesn't match the resolver's workspace, I'll return an error for unsupported cross-workspace routing. This means passing workspace_slug into the resolver and setting it from the session store's workspace_slug.

Now I'm moving on to handle Windows atomic file replacement in the store helpers, and then addressing the immutability constraint for finished workspaces.

The core issue is that if mutations can still occur after a finish, returning a stale finish record is problematic. But if I make finished workspaces immutable—rejecting any mutation when a finish record exists—then returning the existing finish is safe and idempotent. The tricky part is that external authorities like ticket state can change independently, so a finish could become stale even without local mutations. The finding focuses on workflow mutation APIs specifically, so blocking local mutations should be sufficient.

I'm going with immutability: once a workspace is finished, all mutation APIs reject. This is the cleanest invariant to enforce and document. Now I need to identify which mutation APIs need this guard—things like add_node, update_node, link edges, promote, set status—and add a check to prevent mutations on finished workspaces.

Let me start by finding all the runtime-context mutation methods that need this protection, then move on to the CLI hierarchy and other fixes, testing incrementally as I go.

The lock file gets created at `<workspace_dir>/.context.lock`, and if it already exists, another mutation is in progress so I'll return a conflict error or retry with brief backoff. On drop, the lock file gets removed to release the lock. This prevents concurrent mutations from silently overwriting each other.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm looking at the existing test structure and realizing that the test `workflow_finish_enforces_gates_and_is_idempotent` passes a validation gate for `val-session-workflow-finish` with outcome `passed` and expects finish to succeed, but the workflow itself doesn't actually have a validation node with kind=Validation and required — it only has an Action node and a Checkpoint node.

Since `resolve_validation_gates` only queries required specs derived from workflow validation nodes, and there are none, the required_specs is empty, so it just returns the caller-provided gate as-is. The finish loop then checks if any gate is required with a non-passed outcome, and since the caller gate has required=true and outcome=passed, it succeeds. data_json.content:
All remediation work is complete and validated. Here is the summary.

## Session Bootstrap Correctness Remediation — Complete

Resumed durable session `448a0119` (new run `77c3f3b4`, linked to predecessor `9484f7af`).

### Index reconciliation
`ticket scan --force` resolved the reported inconsistency — epic [effba966 [session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping…](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8/ticket.toml) now reports **0 health findings**, and the completed implementation tickets read `done` consistently.

### Remediation ticket
Created [6b1edff1 [session-api][remediation] Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, CLI contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml); linked the epic and action-8 [b4a8dc5e](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6/ticket.toml) to depend on it. Now at **in-review**; board checked out.

### Fixes (all in `memory-api` submodule)
1. **Authoritative validation** — `resolve_validation_gates` now *always* derives required outcomes from test-api; caller outcomes never certify. Fails closed on unknown spec / absent / failed / blocked executions.
2. **Live ticket authority** — `node_is_effectively_done` derives ticket-node completion exclusively from live terminal state; local `Done` is cache-only.
3. **Missing/misrouted state fails closed** — default resolver returns an error for absent tickets and rejects cross-workspace URN routing; `finish_workflow` blocks on ticket-state diagnostics with an explicit "unavailable" reason.
4. **Atomic Windows durability** — `write_json` now moves the old file to a backup and only deletes it after a successful rename, restoring on failure. Added a `create_new` lock-file guard (with stale reclaim) so concurrent mutations cannot silently overwrite (`RuntimeMutationConflict`).
5. **Post-finish immutability** — new `WorkspaceFinished` guard rejects all pin/workflow mutations after finish; finish re-checks under the lock.
6. **Evidence + CLI hierarchy** — added nested `session workflow <subcommand>` with flat `workflow-*` aliases; recorded all evidence.

### Tests — 119 passed
`session-api` (101), `session-cli` (15), `session-mcp` (3) all green, including adversarial regressions (caller-`passed`-vs-authoritative-failed/missing, local-Done-vs-non-terminal-live, production-path missing/misrouted ticket, post-finish immutability, concurrency lock, nested/flat CLI equivalence).

### Evidence traceability
All six spec-cited execution IDs now resolve under [.test/default/executions](.test/default/executions). Added `val-session-cli-suite` / `val-session-mcp-suite` specs. Updated spec [c677182e Durable session workflow graph and handoff continuity](.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ec3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/spec.toml) positions, guards, and traceability; rescanned.

⚠️ **Note:** `test-api record_execution` prunes to 2 runs per spec, which removed the previously *untracked* `exec-session-cli-suite`/`exec-session-mcp-suite` files — I re-recorded both from the passing runs so the cited IDs resolve again.

### Durable workflow
5 remediation nodes → `done`; `action-8` remains `pending`. `session finish` is correctly **blocked** ("required node action-8 is not done") — epic stays open. Handoff `77cabc32` persisted with `val-session-workflow-finish outcome=passed` (authoritative).

### Left untouched (per handoff guidance)
The 6 pre-existing unrelated execution deletions (`exec-60a2a388…`, `exec-recompute…`, etc.) remain **unstaged** — not caused by this work.

### Suggested checkpoint
The `memory-api` submodule holds actions 1–7 plus this remediation. Suggested commit (deepest-first, excluding the 6 unrelated deletions):
`feat(session-api): authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and nested CLI contract`

Action 8 remains blocked until you choose to start it; every preceding required node is complete and authoritative finish is verified as gate-closed.

data_json.content:
**Additional Audit Findings**

I ran `audit-cli` against:

- The complete memory-api repository
- session-api
- session-cli
- session-mcp

The package-level runs are the useful results because the repository-level run incorrectly reported “No Cargo.toml found” for compiler, test, and coverage trials.

1. **High: the main session files substantially exceed the 400-line audit threshold.**

   - store.rs: 2,120 lines
   - store_tests.rs: 2,093 lines
   - session-cli/lib.rs: 1,193 lines
   - session-mcp/server.rs: 1,184 lines

   The audit recommends splitting these into feature-focused modules. This reinforces the review concern around the finish and locking implementation: the controlling state machine is concentrated inside an oversized store module.

2. **Medium: additional changed session-api files exceed the line threshold.**

   - model.rs: 543 lines
   - store_helpers.rs: 462 lines

   These are maintainability findings, not immediate correctness failures.

3. **Medium: `finish_workflow` exceeds the complexity threshold.**

   store.rs has cyclomatic complexity **18**, versus the configured maximum of **12**.

   This is directly relevant to the remaining finish-vs-mutation race. Extracting locked evaluation, ticket completion checks, validation resolution, and certificate persistence into smaller helpers would make the concurrency invariant easier to test.

4. **Medium: CLI dispatch exceeds the complexity threshold.**

   lib.rs has cyclomatic complexity **22**, versus **12**.

   The nested and compatibility command paths work, but the audit recommends splitting dispatch into smaller handlers. This is quality debt rather than an action-8 blocker by itself.

5. **Medium: `latest_session_id` exceeds the complexity threshold.**

   store.rs has complexity **13**, versus **12**. This appears unrelated to the remediation behavior despite living in a changed file.

6. **Coverage evidence is unavailable for all three packages.**

   The audit’s `cargo-llvm-cov` trial failed while compiling each package:

   - `session-api`: no percentage produced
   - `session-cli`: no percentage produced
   - `session-mcp`: no percentage produced

   The audit output truncates before the decisive compiler error, so this is an evidence gap, not proof of inadequate coverage. The ordinary test and compiler trials succeeded.

7. **Audit ticket-graph output conflicts with authoritative ticket health.**

   The repository audit reports [6b1edff1 [session-api][remediation] Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml) as orphaned. Ticket-api health reports zero epic findings, and the ticket has incoming epic/action-8 relationships. Treat this as an audit graph/index discrepancy, not a confirmed ticket defect.

**Passing Audit Trials**

| Package | Compiler warnings | Tests | Static parsing |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `session-api` | 0 | 101/101 | 320 functions, 0 parse failures |
| `session-cli` | 0 | 15/15 | 39 functions, 0 parse failures |
| `session-mcp` | 0 | 3/3 | 45 functions, 0 parse failures |

`session-mcp` has no complexity violations; its maximum is 7. No tracked audit artifacts were modified.

**Updated Verdict**

The audit adds maintainability and coverage-evidence findings but does not displace the correctness blockers from the manual review. Action 8 should remain blocked primarily by:

1. The finish-versus-mutation locking race.
2. Unlocked init/resume lineage updates.
3. The Windows crash interval during backup promotion.
4. Three unresolved runtime-spec execution references.
5. Failed formatting checks.

The audit’s `finish_workflow` complexity finding strengthens the recommendation to repair the locking invariant through smaller, independently testable helpers rather than adding more branches to the existing function.

events.reasoning_text: I found the core issue: `init_runtime_context` doesn't acquire the runtime lock or validate that the workspace is still active, which means init and resume operations are mutating the runtime context—specifically the lineage tracking and active run ID—without synchronization. This creates a race condition where these unlocked mutations can collide with the locked mutations happening elsewhere. I need to examine how `resume_workspace_context` and `finish_workflow` handle this to understand the full scope of the problem.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm identifying the core correctness issues with the locking mechanism. The first blocker involves a potential race between `finish_workflow` and mutations: while there's an initial check for the finish record before acquiring the lock (which is just an optimization), the real concern is that `finish_workflow` reads the context under lock, verifies all nodes are complete, and writes the finish record—but I need to trace through exactly where the vulnerability lies.

The second blocker is the actual race condition: mutations call `ensure_workspace_not_finished` to check if the finish record exists, but this check happens outside the lock. So a mutation thread can pass this check before the finish thread writes its record, then later acquire the lock and mutate the workspace after finish has already marked it complete. The fix is to move the `ensure_workspace_not_finished` check inside the lock (right after acquiring it) so the finish-record validation and the mutation become atomic with respect to the finish thread's lock hold.

There's also an issue with `init_runtime_context` — it reads the context, appends runs, updates the active run ID, and persists all of this without holding the lock. This races against both locked mutations and the finish operation. I need to acquire the lock around that entire read-modify-write sequence. The trickier question is whether init should also check if the workspace is finished. Semantically, creating a new run on a finished workspace is a mutation, and the immutability invariant says finished workspaces shouldn't change. But resume-after-finish might be a legitimate use case. For now I'll focus on adding the lock to init — that's what the audit is flagging. I'll skip the `ensure_workspace_not_finished` check in init to avoid breaking resume semantics, but the core fix is acquiring the lock.

There's a subtlety though: `acquire_runtime_lock` itself calls `runtime_paths_for_workspace` and creates the workspace directory. For the initial creation path, the workspace directory won't exist yet, but `acquire_runtime_lock` handles that with `create_dir_all`. So wrapping the entire read-modify-write in the lock should work for both creation and updates.

Now there's a crash window issue in the Windows backup promotion path — between the rename of the original file to backup and the rename of the temp file to the destination, if a crash occurs the destination won't exist.  The backup file gets orphaned and `read_json` returns NotFound, losing the durable state. To fix this safely, either `read_json` needs to check for a backup if the primary is missing, or we should use an atomic replace operation like Windows' `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, which Rust's `fs::rename` actually leverages on Windows. The comment in the code is actually wrong — Rust's `std::fs::rename` on Windows does use `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, so it atomically replaces existing files. This means the fix is simply to call `fs::rename` directly on the temp file without the backup-swap dance, which eliminates the crash window entirely. On Windows, `MoveFileEx` with `REPLACE_EXISTING` is atomic, so the crash window closes with a simple `fs::rename` on both platforms. I'm confident this has been stable in Rust's standard library for a long time, so I'll unify the code and remove the backup dance entirely.

Now I'm hitting blocker 4: the runtime spec references three execution IDs that don't resolve. Earlier the summary claimed all six resolved, so I need to dig into which spec this is and verify those execution IDs against the test store. Then there's blocker 5 about formatting checks that need to pass.

Looking at the scope, I should prioritize the five correctness blockers first. The maintainability refactoring—splitting files and extracting helpers from finish_workflow—is important but secondary. The user's verdict flags those five blockers as what should gate action 8, so let me focus there, though extracting finish_workflow helpers is worth doing since it ties directly to the locking invariant.

I'll be pragmatic: tackle the five correctness blockers, starting with moving `ensure_workspace_not_finished` inside the lock.

The key insight is reordering the checks—acquire the lock first, then verify the workspace isn't finished. This way, the finish-check happens atomically with the lock hold. When `finish_workflow` writes the finish record under the lock, any mutation that acquires the lock afterward will see it and reject. But there's still a potential race: a mutation acquires the lock, checks that no finish record exists, then releases the lock to do its write. Meanwhile, finish waits for the lock, acquires it, reads the context (which now includes the mutation's write), evaluates, and writes the finish record. The mutation's write and the finish record both end up in the context.

Actually, that's acceptable—finish saw the mutation's effect. The problematic direction is the reverse: finish writes the record first, then a mutation proceeds. By swapping the order so the lock is acquired before the not_finished check, I close that race.

Now I need to find all the mutation call sites and swap the order, add locking to `init_runtime_context`, simplify the Windows JSON write to use atomic rename, verify the three execution references, and run `cargo fmt`.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm creating a helper function that acquires the runtime mutation lock first, then checks whether the workspace has finished—consolidating the pattern of locking before verifying state.

The key insight is that the finished-check must happen after holding the lock to prevent a race where a finish commits while a mutation is in flight. Any mutation that acquires the lock after finish will see the finish record and be rejected.

For mutation sites like unpin, I'll do the pure validation (parsing the entity URN) before acquiring the lock to fail fast, then acquire the lock and proceed with the actual mutation.

The finish workflow itself acquires the lock, checks the finish record under lock, and releases it when done. If a mutation tries to proceed during finish, it gets a RuntimeMutationConflict error rather than silently succeeding—fail-closed behavior is acceptable here.

Now I need to think through init_runtime_context locking similarly, considering whether init should also check the finished state. I need to acquire the lock early in init_runtime_context to serialize all mutations, then add a finished-state check that blocks init/resume when they'd mutate an already-finished workspace — though the initial workspace creation and non-mutating re-inits should still be allowed.

Writing the implementation... ates the immutability invariant, so I need to check if the workspace is finished before allowing new runs or predecessor references. I'm identifying the third blocker: on Windows, the backup-swap approach creates a crash window where the destination file is temporarily missing between the rename-to-backup and rename-tmp-to-dest operations. Since Windows's `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING` handles atomic replacement natively, I can simplify this to a single atomic rename call that works consistently across both platforms, eliminating the vulnerability.

Replacing the platform-specific conditional blocks...

data_json.content: Now blocker 3: the Windows backup-swap introduces a crash window (destination absent between the two renames). Rust's `fs::rename` on Windows uses `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, which atomically replaces the destination — so the backup dance is both unnecessary and the source of the crash interval. Collapse to a single atomic rename.

First, one that verifies a finished workspace properly rejects attempts to create a resume run with force_new_run enabled, returning a WorkspaceFinished error. The key insight is that with the new lock-first ordering, if both a live lock and a finish record exist, the mutation should fail with RuntimeMutationConflict (because the lock acquisition fails before we even check if the workspace is finished). This is different from the old behavior where it would return WorkspaceFinished. So I can write a test that manually holds a lock while calling a mutation on a finished workspace—if it returns RuntimeMutationConflict instead of WorkspaceFinished, that proves the lock is being acquired before the finished check. That's a solid regression test for the ordering fix. Writing test assertions...

arguments.description:
# Session bootstrap correctness remediation

Repairs correctness holes found during independent review of the durable
session-bootstrap implementation (actions 1–7) before action 8 or epic closure.

## Findings to repair (in order)

1. **Authoritative validation** — `resolve_validation_gates` lets a caller-provided
   gate outcome win when present in `by_id`. Required guard outcomes must always come
   from test-api; caller payloads may identify/display gates but never certify them.
   Fail closed for unknown specs, absent, failed, or blocked executions.
2. **Live ticket authority** — `node_is_effectively_done` returns true for any locally
   `Done` node before checking whether it is ticket-backed. Ticket-backed nodes must
   derive completion exclusively from authoritative live terminal state; local status is
   display/cache only.
3. **Missing/misrouted ticket state fails open** — default resolver returns `Ok(None)`
   for absent tickets and the URN workspace segment is ignored. Treat missing required
   ticket state as unavailable (block finish); route cross-workspace URNs or reject
   unsupported routing explicitly.
4. **Windows atomic replacement** — the Windows branch deletes the destination before
   renaming the temp file, losing durable state on crash. Preserve the old file until
   replacement succeeds; enforce concurrent-mutation safety.
5. **Stale finish** — `finish_workflow` returns an existing `finish.json` before
   reevaluating authorities, and mutation APIs remain available afterward. Choose and
   enforce one invariant (immutable-after-finish OR mutation invalidates finish).
6. **Evidence traceability** — spec-cited `exec-val-session-*` IDs have no persisted
   execution records. Record required validations through test-api; reconcile cited IDs.
7. **CLI contract** — expose nested `session workflow ...` hierarchy with flat forms as
   compatibility aliases; add parsing/integration tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions; caller
  `passed` cannot produce a successful finish (regression test).
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state; locally `Done` + non-terminal
  live state rejects finish (regression test).
- Missing/failed/misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish (production-path tests).
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails; concurrent updates
  cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence; unrelated execution
  deletions are explained or excluded.
- Focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.

## Audit follow-up (2026-07-14)

A second-pass `audit-cli` review surfaced deeper correctness issues on the same
finish/locking/durability scope. Repaired:

- **Finish-vs-mutation race** — the finished-check ran before the mutation lock, so a
  mutation that observed "not finished" could commit after `finish_workflow` wrote its
  record. All runtime mutations now go through `begin_runtime_mutation`, which acquires
  the lock first and evaluates the finished-check under the lock. Regression:
  `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock`.
- **Unlocked init/resume lineage** — `init_runtime_context`/`resume_workspace_context`
  appended run lineage without the mutation lock and without an immutability guard. They
  now acquire the lock and reject run creation on a finished workspace. Regression:
  `finished_workspace_rejects_resume_run_creation`.
- **Windows crash interval** — the backup-swap dance left the destination absent between
  two renames. Replaced with a single `fs::rename` (atomic `MoveFileExW` replace-existing
  on Windows, `rename(2)` on Unix), removing the crash window.
- **`finish_workflow` complexity** — extracted `existing_finish_result`,
  `evaluate_workflow_completion`, and `evaluate_required_validation` so the locked finish
  path is a linear sequence and the completion invariant is independently testable.
- **Three unresolved runtime-spec executions** — `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`,
  `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, and `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`
  (cited by spec 709f067a) were removed by an earlier `prune_execution_runs` side-effect
  and are re-recorded from passing runs; all now resolve.
- **Formatting** — `cargo fmt` applied to session-api/session-cli/session-mcp; check is clean.

Validation: `cargo test -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp` — 121 passed, 0 failed.
Maintainability findings (file-size splits for store.rs/store_tests.rs/lib.rs/server.rs,
CLI dispatch complexity, coverage-evidence gap) are noted as follow-up quality debt and do
not block action 8.

## Traceability

- Epic: effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8
- Blocks action 8: b4a8dc5e-9d80-4fea-bb42-0c30aba0ecd6
- Spec: 8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913 (parent contract)
- Spec: c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf (workflow/finish contract)
- Spec: 709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf (runtime-context contract)
- Validation: val-session-workflow-finish

# Session bootstrap correctness remediation

Repairs correctness holes found during independent review of the durable
session-bootstrap implementation (actions 1–7) before action 8 or epic closure.

## Findings to repair (in order)

1. **Authoritative validation** — `resolve_validation_gates` lets a caller-provided
   gate outcome win when present in `by_id`. Required guard outcomes must always come
   from test-api; caller payloads may identify/display gates but never certify them.
   Fail closed for unknown specs, absent, failed, or blocked executions.
2. **Live ticket authority** — `node_is_effectively_done` returns true for any locally
   `Done` node before checking whether it is ticket-backed. Ticket-backed nodes must
   derive completion exclusively from authoritative live terminal state; local status is
   display/cache only.
3. **Missing/misrouted ticket state fails open** — default resolver returns `Ok(None)`
   for absent tickets and the URN workspace segment is ignored. Treat missing required
   ticket state as unavailable (block finish); route cross-workspace URNs or reject
   unsupported routing explicitly.
4. **Windows atomic replacement** — the Windows branch deletes the destination before
   renaming the temp file, losing durable state on crash. Preserve the old file until
   replacement succeeds; enforce concurrent-mutation safety.
5. **Stale finish** — `finish_workflow` returns an existing `finish.json` before
   reevaluating authorities, and mutation APIs remain available afterward. Choose and
   enforce one invariant (immutable-after-finish OR mutation invalidates finish).
6. **Evidence traceability** — spec-cited `exec-val-session-*` IDs have no persisted
   execution records. Record required validations through test-api; reconcile cited IDs.
7. **CLI contract** — expose nested `session workflow ...` hierarchy with flat forms as
   compatibility aliases; add parsing/integration tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- Required validation outcomes come only from authoritative test-api executions; caller
  `passed` cannot produce a successful finish (regression test).
- Ticket-backed nodes require live terminal ticket state; locally `Done` + non-terminal
  live state rejects finish (regression test).
- Missing/failed/misrouted ticket resolution blocks finish (production-path tests).
- Post-finish mutation behavior is explicit, implemented, and tested.
- Durable writes preserve the previous file if replacement fails; concurrent updates
  cannot silently overwrite each other.
- Canonical nested CLI commands and compatibility aliases are tested.
- Every spec-cited execution ID resolves to persisted evidence; unrelated execution
  deletions are explained or excluded.
- Focused and full session-api, CLI, and MCP validations pass.

## Audit follow-up (2026-07-14)

A second-pass `audit-cli` review surfaced deeper correctness issues on the same
finish/locking/durability scope. Repaired:

- **Finish-vs-mutation race** — the finished-check ran before the mutation lock, so a
  mutation that observed "not finished" could commit after `finish_workflow` wrote its
  record. All runtime mutations now go through `begin_runtime_mutation`, which acquires
  the lock first and evaluates the finished-check under the lock. Regression:
  `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock`.
- **Unlocked init/resume lineage** — `init_runtime_context`/`resume_workspace_context`
  appended run lineage without the mutation lock and without an immutability guard. They
  now acquire the lock and reject run creation on a finished workspace. Regression:
  `finished_workspace_rejects_resume_run_creation`.
- **Windows crash interval** — the backup-swap dance left the destination absent between
  two renames. Replaced with a single `fs::rename` (atomic `MoveFileExW` replace-existing
  on Windows, `rename(2)` on Unix), removing the crash window.
- **`finish_workflow` complexity** — extracted `existing_finish_result`,
  `evaluate_workflow_completion`, and `evaluate_required_validation` so the locked finish
  path is a linear sequence and the completion invariant is independently testable.
- **Three unresolved runtime-spec executions** — `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`,
  `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, and `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`
  (cited by spec 709f067a) were removed by an earlier `prune_execution_runs` side-effect
  and are re-recorded from passing runs; all now resolve.
- **Formatting** — `cargo fmt` applied to session-api/session-cli/session-mcp; check is clean.

Validation: `cargo test -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp` — 121 passed, 0 failed.
Maintainability findings (file-size splits for store.rs/store_tests.rs/lib.rs/server.rs,
CLI dispatch complexity, coverage-evidence gap) are noted as follow-up quality debt and do
not block action 8.

arguments.summary: Repaired the audit's five correctness blockers in session-api: (1) closed the finish-vs-mutation race by acquiring the lock before the finished-check via a new begin_runtime_mutation helper; (2) lock-guarded init/resume lineage and made it immutable after finish; (3) removed the Windows backup-swap crash window in favor of a single atomic rename; (4) extracted finish_workflow into smaller testable helpers to cut complexity; (5) re-recorded the three pruned runtime-spec executions so all spec-cited IDs resolve; and fixed the formatting check. Added two regression tests. Full validation: 121 tests pass across session-api/session-cli/session-mcp, fmt clean. Updated spec c677182e durability wording and the remediation ticket's evidence trail. Maintainability/coverage findings noted as non-blocking follow-up.

data_json.content:
I addressed the audit's correctness blockers. All are repaired, validated, and the evidence trail is reconciled.

## What I changed

**1. Finish-vs-mutation locking race** — The finished-check ran *before* the mutation lock, so a mutation that read "not finished" could commit after `finish_workflow` wrote its record. Added `begin_runtime_mutation` (acquire lock, *then* check finished under the lock) and routed all six mutation entry points through it. Regression test `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock` proves the ordering (a finished workspace with a live lock returns `RuntimeMutationConflict`, not `WorkspaceFinished`).

**2. Unlocked init/resume lineage** — `init_runtime_context`/`resume_workspace_context` appended run lineage with no lock and no immutability guard. They now acquire the mutation lock and reject run creation on a finished workspace. Regression: `finished_workspace_rejects_resume_run_creation`.

**3. Windows crash interval** — The backup-swap dance left the destination absent between two renames. Its premise was wrong: Rust's `fs::rename` maps to `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, which atomically replaces. Collapsed to a single atomic rename on both platforms, removing the crash window.

**4. `finish_workflow` complexity (18 → lower)** — Extracted `existing_finish_result`, `evaluate_workflow_completion`, and `evaluate_required_validation`, per the audit's recommendation to make the locking invariant testable through smaller helpers rather than more branches in one function.

**5. Three unresolved runtime-spec executions** — `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, and `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714` (cited by spec `709f067a`) had been pruned by an earlier `prune_execution_runs` side-effect. Re-recorded all three from passing runs; verified they resolve. The six `c677182e` executions were unaffected (per-spec pruning).

**6. Formatting** — `cargo fmt` applied to the three session crates; `--check` is now clean.

data.content:
I addressed the audit's correctness blockers. All are repaired, validated, and the evidence trail is reconciled.

## What I changed

**1. Finish-vs-mutation locking race** — The finished-check ran *before* the mutation lock, so a mutation that read "not finished" could commit after `finish_workflow` wrote its record. Added `begin_runtime_mutation` (acquire lock, *then* check finished under the lock) and routed all six mutation entry points through it. Regression test `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock` proves the ordering (a finished workspace with a live lock returns `RuntimeMutationConflict`, not `WorkspaceFinished`).

**2. Unlocked init/resume lineage** — `init_runtime_context`/`resume_workspace_context` appended run lineage with no lock and no immutability guard. They now acquire the mutation lock and reject run creation on a finished workspace. Regression: `finished_workspace_rejects_resume_run_creation`.

**3. Windows crash interval** — The backup-swap dance left the destination absent between two renames. Its premise was wrong: Rust's `fs::rename` maps to `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, which atomically replaces. Collapsed to a single atomic rename on both platforms, removing the crash window.

**4. `finish_workflow` complexity (18 → lower)** — Extracted `existing_finish_result`, `evaluate_workflow_completion`, and `evaluate_required_validation`, per the audit's recommendation to make the locking invariant testable through smaller helpers rather than more branches in one function.

**5. Three unresolved runtime-spec executions** — `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, and `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714` (cited by spec `709f067a`) had been pruned by an earlier `prune_execution_runs` side-effect. Re-recorded all three from passing runs; verified they resolve. The six `c677182e` executions were unaffected (per-spec pruning).

**6. Formatting** — `cargo fmt` applied to the three session crates; `--check` is now clean.

Updated spec `c677182e` to describe the corrected atomic-rename durability and under-lock finished-check, rescanned the spec store (909 integrated, 0 diagnostics), and recorded the audit follow-up on ticket [6b1edff1 Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml).

**Validation:** `cargo test -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp` → **121 passed, 0 failed** (2 new regressions); formatting check clean.

The maintainability findings (file-size splits for `store.rs`/`store_tests.rs`/`lib.rs`/`server.rs`, CLI dispatch complexity 22, `latest_session_id` complexity 13, coverage-evidence gap) remain as follow-up quality debt and do not block action 8. Audit finding #7 (graph shows the ticket orphaned) is an audit index discrepancy — ticket-api reports the epic/action-8 edges intact — so no ticket change was needed.

Suggested commit checkpoint (memory-api submodule): `fix(session-api): close finish/mutation race, lock init/resume lineage, atomic durable writes; reconcile runtime-spec evidence`.
